signals.raw:
South Korea's March Inflation Rises to 2.2%, Exceeding Central Bank Target | YES | HIGH confidence**

South Korea's annual inflation rate **rose to 2.2% in March 2026**, up from 2.0% in the previous two months but below market forecasts of 2.4%.[1] This marks a significant development as it represents the first increase above the Bank of Korea's 2% target in recent months, driven largely by **surg
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: Forward Outlook:**
signals.raw:
Forward Outlook:**

South Korea's inflation is expected to reach 2.60% by the end of Q2 2026,[1] with international forecasters projecting full-year 2026 inflation between 2.3% and 2.7%.[6] The central bank has signaled its easing cycle has ended, with the policy rate likely to remain at 2.5% for an extended period despite moderating growth conditions.[2]
